items.title: Watch: Trump speaks at G7 summit after threatening Iran
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday at the G7 conference that the U.S. will "go right back to dropping bombs" if he doesn't like the Iran deal.

items.title: Less than one-third of global workers feel their jobs are safe, ADP survey data shows
items.summary: Even with global unemployment at historically low levels, workers are not feeling secure.

items.title: Bhartiya Urban will invest Rs 1,000 crore in developing 1.5 million sq ft residential development in North Bengaluru
items.summary: Bhartiya Urban is launching Nikoo Homes 8 in Bengaluru's Thanisandra corridor. This project spans 11 acres and includes over 1,000 homes. It offers a range of options from studios to luxury villas. The development has a business potential of Rs 2000 crore. Bhartiya Urban plans further launches and inventory additions across multiple locations.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Indian equities advanced for a fourth straight session as easing geopolitical tensions, lower crude oil prices, softer bond yields and a stronger rupee boosted sentiment. Gains in IT, metals and PSU banks lifted benchmarks, while India VIX remained subdued amid improving market confidence.

items.title: Allbirds continues AI pivot with name change and CEO hire, sending stock soaring
items.summary: Allbirds became NewBird AI in April and said it would trade shoes for AI compute infrastructure.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold and silver remain steady ahead of US Fed policy decision
items.summary: Precious metals showed little movement as traders awaited the US Federal Reserve's interest-rate decision. Gold futures rose slightly to $4,369, while silver gained to over $70, amid geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns following potential US-Iran peace talks.

items.title: Amazon AI exec predicts first 'commercially useful' quantum computers in 5-7 years
items.summary: Quantum computing is becoming an increasingly competitive field with tech giants like Microsoft, Google and IBM developing the technology.

items.title: Driving test wait time target will not be met until autumn next year
items.summary: The Transport Secretary was aiming to reduce the backlog to seven weeks by the autumn.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Titagarh Rail among 2 stock recommendations for Thursday
items.summary: Indian equities extended their recovery amid easing US-Iran tensions and softer crude prices. Analysts recommended Titagarh Rail Systems and Engineers India, citing bullish technical patterns, strong momentum, improving volumes and favorable positioning above key moving averages for potential near-term gains.

items.title: India’s electronics output hits ₹13 lakh crore; Vaishnaw eyes No. 2 export spot
items.summary: India's electronics production has reached Rs 13 lakh crore. The nation aims to make electronics the second largest export category. Two semiconductor plants are already operational. Two more chip plants will begin full commercial production this year. This growth is driven by smartphone exports, with Apple being a major contributor.

items.title: SpaceX surge is creating a unique hedging opportunity
items.summary: Tuesday's launch of SpaceX options set new records for a first-day, post-IPO options volume.

items.title: Fed Chair Warsh expected to withhold 'dot' from central bank's interest rate outlook
items.summary: The central bank's Federal Open Market Committee is set to release its quarterly update of where individual officials expect interest rates to head.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz threat level downgraded after Iran deal, says U.S.-led maritime security group
items.summary: The behavior of Iranian forces "has become less volatile" and the U.S. Navy continues to provide oversight, the Joint Maritime Information Center said.

items.title: Wall Street edges higher ahead of Federal Reserve rate decision, Broadcom jumps 4.4%, Applied Materials surges 8.6%
items.summary: At 10:15 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 gained 0.2%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average advanced 0.5%. The Nasdaq Composite also rose 0.2%

items.title: BRND.ME transitions to a public company ahead of planned IPO
items.summary: The conversion into a public company brings BRND.ME under a structure that is more aligned with public-market requirements, enabling greater governance clarity and regulatory alignment

items.title: InCred Capital expands global footprint with launch of US operations; appoints Arunava Das as CEO, North America
items.summary: InCred Capital, the institutional, wealth and asset management arm of InCred Group, announced the launch of its US operations with the opening of its New York office.

items.title: Civil aviation min to upgrade AirSewa portal, tap AI for faster passengers' grievances redressal
items.summary: The Civil Aviation Ministry is enhancing the AirSewa portal and integrating artificial intelligence into the Passenger Assistance Control Room. This initiative aims to address air passenger grievances swiftly and effectively. The Passenger Assistance Control Room has already resolved over 73,000 complaints with a high success rate. Future upgrades will ensure a more seamless platform for passenger support.

items.title: 'A signal of where power sits': Trump and world leaders joined by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google at G7
items.summary: Frontier AI risks, infrastructure and sovereignty are all expected to be discussed at the world leaders' summit.

items.title: SpaceX stock sinks 5%, losing momentum after a multiday rally
items.summary: Elon Musk's space and AI company has seen its stock surge since its blockbuster IPO on Friday.

items.title: UK-India free trade deal to kick in on July 15, cutting tariffs by over $480 mn in year one
items.summary: The United Kingdom and India will implement their Free Trade Agreement on July 15. This landmark deal, signed last year, promises to boost trade significantly. Businesses have 28 days to prepare for the changes. Tariffs on many UK exports to India will be reduced, including Scotch whisky and automotive products. India will also eliminate tariffs on most UK imports.

items.title: SEBI plugs long-standing gap in AIF winding-up process
items.summary: The framework allows AIFs to retain liquidation proceeds in specified cases, including pending or anticipated litigation, tax or regulatory demands, and residual winding-up expenses

items.title: EV industry seeks financing, payment security support to drive adoption
items.summary: Electric vehicle makers are asking the government for better financing and payment security. This is to speed up the use of electric vehicles and boost local production. Industry leaders met with Union Minister HD Kumaraswamy to discuss challenges. They also want more charging stations and faster approvals for new technologies. The minister assured support for the EV sector's growth.

items.title: Middle East peace deal could herald oil glut next year, says IEA
items.summary: ‘Gradual’ return of flows will give way to sharp rise in production, outstripping forecasts for increased demand

items.title: HDFC MF, ADIA among buyers as Sepia Investments offloads Rs 749 crore in Corona Remedies via block deal
items.summary: Sepia Investments and Anchor Partners sold shares worth about Rs 777 crore in Corona Remedies through block deals. HDFC Mutual Fund emerged as the largest buyer, while several institutional investors, including Aberdeen entities, ADIA, Invesco and WhiteOak, also accumulated stakes.

items.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to do fodder planning to avoid a potential shortage due to El Nino
items.summary: Maharashtra farmers are urged to plan for fodder shortages due to El Nino. The Animal Husbandry Department advises cultivating nutritious, multipurpose fodder crops. Farmers should also preserve natural grass and store excess fodder as silage. This proactive approach aims to prevent a drop in milk production and ensure livestock health during potential feed scarcity from February to June 2027.

items.title: IPO comeback: ₹60,000 crore pipeline in next three months
items.summary: This resurgence follows a six-month slowdown, driven by easing geopolitical tensions, a rally in equity markets, and declining crude prices.

items.title: Govt's 5% GIC Re offer for sale fully subscribed; boosts FY27 divestment drive
items.summary: Strong investor response triggered the full greenshoe option, despite a sharp 9% drop in the state-owned reinsurer's share price during the transaction.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions IPO to open on Friday: 10 key things to know from RHP before you subscribe to ₹883 crore
items.summary: Turtlemint's IPO opens for subscription from June 19 to June 23, aiming to raise ₹882.67 crore. The company reserves 75% for QIBs, 15% for NIIs, and 10% for retail investors, with shares priced between ₹144-155. Listing is expected on June 29.

items.title: Target Corp leases 8.3 lakh sq ft office space in Bengaluru for 10 years
items.summary: US retail giant Target Corporation secures a significant office space in Bengaluru. The company will occupy over 8.3 lakh square feet at Embassy Manyata Business Park. This long-term lease, valued at approximately Rs 1,250 crore, highlights the growing presence of global capability centers in India's tech hub. The deal underscores sustained demand for Grade A office spaces in Bengaluru.

items.title: Rupee hits six-week high on RBI measures and dollar inflow hopes
items.summary: The rupee extended its gains to close at 94.53 against the US dollar, supported by RBI measures to boost foreign currency inflows, softer crude prices and expectations of stronger FCNR-B deposit inflows. The currency touched a six-week high before trimming intraday gains.

items.title: CME chief Duffy to step down after reshaping global derivatives trading
items.summary: CEO led the group through a transformation from markets dominated by physical pits to decentralised electronic trading

items.title: Telecom tower companies eye 5–6% tenancy growth on network push
items.summary: Telecom tower companies anticipate strong tenancy growth of five to six percent in the coming two fiscal years. This surge is fueled by telecom operators expanding their networks. This revival will boost tenancy ratios at existing sites. Tower companies will invest in new towers and upgrades. This investment will improve operational efficiency and profitability for the sector.

items.title: Treasury yields are little changed as investors await Fed interest rates decision
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields were little changed on Wednesday as investors await the outcome of Kevin Warsh's first Federal Reserve policy meeting.

items.title: Digital systems secure, says Axis Bank amid AI platform-driven vulnerability claims on cybersecurity
items.summary: Axis Bank assures its digital systems are secure. The bank states cyber fraud largely occurs when customers are tricked by external communications, not due to weaknesses in its own cybersecurity. Officials highlighted that advanced AI models like Mythos can accelerate vulnerability discovery. Axis Bank is enhancing its response to identified weaknesses and deploying new security tools to protect customers.

items.title: US stocks: US market ticks up ahead of first Fed policy decision under Warsh
items.summary: U.S. stock indexes opened higher on Wednesday, with chip stocks rebounding ahead of the Federal Reserve's first interest rate decision under new Chair Kevin Warsh. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all saw gains in early trading.

items.title: Thoma Bravo hands Medallia to lenders in one of PE’s biggest losses
items.summary: Consortium led by Blackstone to take over troubled software company

items.title: BRND.ME converts into public company, eyes IPO in 12-18 months
items.summary: BRND.ME, formerly Mensa Brands, has converted into a public company after shifting its corporate base to India. The consumer brands platform is preparing for a potential IPO within 12-18 months, backed by stronger governance, profitability and a streamlined corporate structure.

items.title: Sebi warns of no regulatory recourse for investors trading in unlisted securities
items.summary: Sebi has cautioned investors against trading unlisted securities through unauthorized websites and electronic platforms. The regulator said such platforms operate outside its oversight, leaving investors without regulatory safeguards, grievance redressal mechanisms or access to investor protection benefits.

items.title: FEMA probe against Apollo Hospitals, 5 directors closed after compounding order: ED
items.summary: A foreign exchange violation case against Apollo Hospitals and five directors has been terminated. The Reserve Bank of India issued a compounding order. Apollo Hospitals Enterprises Limited made a one-time payment of over Rs 17.76 crore. The executives also paid Rs 18 lakh each. This settlement resolves alleged contraventions totalling more than Rs 2,424 crore.

items.title: India-EU free trade deal to be inked by year-end: Ursula von der Leyen
items.summary: India and the European Union are set to sign a major free trade agreement by year-end. This follows talks between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and EU leaders. Security and defense cooperation will also increase. Both sides will advance the India-Middle East-Europe Corridor for better connectivity. This agreement marks a significant step in bilateral relations.

items.title: RBI temporarily eases FCNR(B), NRE deposit rate norms to attract overseas funds
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has eased interest rate rules for overseas deposits. This allows banks to offer better returns on FCNR(B) and NRE accounts. The changes aim to attract more foreign funds until September 2026. This move could boost foreign exchange inflows. It will help strengthen the banking system and manage global market volatility.

items.title: Tesla opens first Telangana experience centre, brings 2026 Model Y range to Hyderabad
items.summary: Tesla has opened its fifth Experience Centre in India, located in Hyderabad. This new facility showcases the 2026 Model Y Premium Rear-Wheel Drive and the Model Y L. Customers can explore these electric vehicles from June 17. Tesla is also offering delivery and after-sales services from its Bollaram facility to support customers in Telangana.

items.title: Broker’s Call: DLF (Buy)
items.summary: The company achieved record collections of ₹13,517 crore, up 15% year on year during FY26 and recorded pre-sales of ₹20,143 crore, in-line with DLF’s guidance

items.title: Indian crypto traders are moving to futures. Here's where the math actually works
items.summary: WazirX Futures brings direct INR access, the lowest fees in the domestic market, and built-in risk guardrails to a segment that has quietly been leaking volume to offshore platforms.

items.title: Quote of the day by Bill Miller: "By the time market declines (or advances) are front-page news, they usually have run their course."
items.summary: Bill Miller’s quote highlights how markets often move ahead of news coverage, leaving latecomers exposed to poor timing. The piece explains why headline-driven investing can be risky, emphasises the role of sentiment, and underlines the importance of independent thinking, discipline and a forward-looking approach in navigating financial markets.

items.title: FED Meeting Live | Dow Jones | US Stock Market Live: SpaceX falls nearly 6%, on track for first loss since blockbuster IPO; US stocks rise
US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures edge higher ahead of Fed decision; oil drops for fifth day
items.summary: US stock futures rose modestly on June 17 as investors awaited the Federal Reserve's policy decision. While the S&P 500 futures remained stable, Nasdaq 100 futures gained 0.6%. The Fed is expected to maintain interest rates amid ongoing inflation concerns related to Middle Eastern tensions.

items.title: Embassy REIT inks ₹1,250 cr mega GCC lease deal with Target
items.summary: The deal is significant as it indicates a massive expansion by Target, which already occupies around 600,000 sq ft in Embassy Manyata in Bengaluru

items.title: Harmony Infra acquires complete ownership of The Horizon Residences in Indirapuram
items.summary: Harmony Infra has acquired full ownership of The Horizon Residences in Indirapuram for Rs 250 crore, exiting the previous promoter and lender. The developer plans to invest an additional Rs 550 crore to complete the ultra-luxury project, expecting Rs 1,600 crore in revenue from 264 residences. This strategic acquisition aims to address the demand for quality, modern developments in Indirapuram.

items.title: Harmony Infra Ventures to invest Rs 800 cr in luxury housing project in Delhi-NCR
items.summary: Harmony Infra Ventures is investing Rs 800 crore in a new luxury housing project called The Horizon Residences in Indirapuram, Ghaziabad. The project will feature 264 units, with prices ranging from Rs 6-7.5 crore. Around 100 units have already been sold. The company expects to complete the project by 2030 and anticipates revenue of Rs 1,600 crore.

items.title: India bonds pause oil-led rally ahead of Fed verdict
items.summary: Indian government bonds saw their rally pause on Wednesday. Oil prices stabilized, and the US Federal Reserve's upcoming policy decision kept investors cautious. The benchmark 10-year bond yield hovered near a 12-week low. Traders are now awaiting the Fed's verdict. Hawkish guidance could impact foreign investment in Indian bonds. Overseas investors have recently poured over $2 billion into domestic bonds.

items.title: Yes Bank among 6 stocks that hit 52-week highs & rallied up to 15% in a month
items.summary: Six BSE 200 stocks, including CG Power, Federal Bank, Yes Bank, Polycab India, Varun Beverages and Pidilite Industries, scaled fresh 52-week highs as the Sensex advanced 347 points.

items.title: Vedanta Iron and Steel aims to become major resource-backed integrated steel platform
items.summary: Vedanta Iron And Steel Ltd, newly listed, plans to be a leading integrated steel producer. The company will leverage its vast iron ore reserves for over 50 years. Expansion will focus on value-added products like silicon iron and ductile iron pipes. VISL's strategy is set to create long-term value across the iron ore and steel chain.

items.title: BMW stock slumps to 5-year low as Iran war and China slowdown spark profit warning
items.summary: BMW blamed a slowdown in its Chinese market, as well as disruption from the Iran war, as two reasons for the slashed guidance.

items.title: US Fed Meeting 2026 LIVE: Kevin Warsh faces first Fed test as Trump pushes for lower rates
items.summary: US Fed Meeting 2026 LIVE: The US central bank is expected to hold the federal funds target steady at a range of 3.50% to 3.75%, marking incoming Chair Kevin Warsh’s first.

items.title: Buying US stocks via Gift City to get easier as Zerodha, Groww, Angel One and Upstox get nod
items.summary: Buying US stocks could soon become easier for Indian retail investors after Zerodha, Groww, Angel One and Upstox secured regulatory approvals from GIFT City’s IFSCA. The licences will enable the brokerages to facilitate overseas investing, tapping into growing demand for global equities under the RBI’s Liberalised Remittance Scheme.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty rise for 4th session on easing crude ahead of US Fed outcome; defence, bank stocks shine
items.summary: Sectoral performance remained mixed, with defence, consumer durables, PSU banks and metal stocks emerging as the top performers

items.title: Rathbones and Investec merger stalled regulatory checks
items.summary: UK wealth manager had to halt investment from high-risk clients as a result of failings

items.title: Paras Defence, to MTAR Tech, HAL: Defence stocks rally as India's defence production touches all-time high
items.summary: India's defence production hit a record ₹1.78 lakh crore in FY26, driving share prices up for defence firms. The sector experienced 15.6% growth from FY25, with increased private sector contributions and strong export performance boosting confidence in the industry's future.

items.title: Raghuram Rajan's warning to India after Hormuz shock: Build bigger oil reserves, diversify faster
items.summary: Global economic shocks from trade disruptions and geopolitical tensions are reshaping resilience, according to Raghuram Rajan. He stresses India's need for larger strategic oil reserves and diversified import/export markets. Rajan also highlights the rupee's depreciation linked to low foreign investment and urges a long-term view on critical commodity exposure, advocating for domestic production and strategic buffers.

items.title: The electric vehicle transition gets heavy
items.summary: Chinese truckmakers spearhead challenge to diesel engine’s supremacy

items.title: Natural gas prices rebound as inventories tighten, LNG exports rise: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: Natural gas prices are climbing back up. This recovery is driven by lower stored gas, increased power sector use, and more US liquefied natural gas exports. Future growth is expected as new export facilities come online. Data centers are also becoming a new source of demand. The market is seeing a significant shift.

items.title: Abu Dhabi hosts India’s travel leaders to strengthen partnerships
items.summary: Abu Dhabi's Department of Culture and Tourism, in partnership with Etihad Airways, hosted a C-level trade visit from India, bringing together senior travel leaders. The visit underscored Abu Dhabi's robust tourism performance, with strong occupancy rates and increased museum visitors, alongside significant air connectivity with India. This collaboration aims to foster long-term value and enhance traveler experiences.

items.title: Manipal Hospitals is said to plan $1 billion IPO in July
items.summary: Manipal Health Enterprises is likely to launch its IPO as early as next month, targeting a valuation of around $10 billion. Backed by Temasek, the hospital chain has completed investor roadshows. The offering includes both fresh issue and stake sale, potentially becoming India’s first billion-dollar IPO this year.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Procter & Gamble Hygiene and Health Care (Long)
items.summary: Amid muted category growth and rising competitive intensity, the stock has undergone a meaningful de-rating and now trades at 32x/30x FY27E/FY28E EPS

items.title: Rupee closes flat at 94.52 vs USD as oil-led rally faces off against dollar demand; Fed verdict looms
items.summary: The Indian rupee closed almost flat against the US dollar on Wednesday. Earlier gains were erased as corporate and importer demand for dollars increased. A significant drop in oil prices had initially boosted the rupee. Market focus now shifts to the US Federal Reserve's policy decision. Commentary from the Fed is expected to guide future interest rate expectations.

items.title: Beat the peers, drop the stock: The brutal reality facing Cognizant CEO Ravi Kumar
items.summary: Cognizant is outgrowing its biggest rivals, yet its stock just hit a six-year low. As OpenAI and Anthropic trigger existential panic, can CEO Ravi Kumar S’s radical pivot to a flatter, AI-first workforce save the IT giant?

items.title: TMPV shares tumble 8% as JLR unveils FY27 growth strategy
items.summary: JLR said the next phase of its Reimagine strategy will focus on expanding customer choice and strengthening the company’s resilience and growth profile

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex gains 347 pts, Nifty closes at 24,085 on easing crude oil prices
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices highlights: India's stock benchmarks rose for a fourth consecutive session ​on Wednesday, their longest winning streak in two months, aided ‌by falling crude oil prices after the U.S.-Iran ​peace deal.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Hyundai Motor India (Buy)
items.summary: HMIL targets exports volume growth of 8-10% for FY27 despite near-term headwinds due to the West Asia crisis, which accounts for 40% of its exports volume

items.title: 'We did it': Modi, Starmer caught on mic hinting at breakthrough in UK-India trade deal
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer were overheard at the G7 summit appearing to confirm a breakthrough in implementing the UK-India trade deal. Their exchange suggests outstanding issues, potentially including steel safeguard measures, have been resolved, paving the way for the agreement's early entry into force.

items.title: The Sameer Gehlaut effect? Why multibagger stock Indiabulls share price has rallied 145% in 3 months
items.summary: Indiabulls Limited has experienced a remarkable comeback, with shares rising 185%. The company restructured its operations, focusing on real estate, and reported substantial profit growth. Fresh capital infusion and increasing promoter ownership signal strong future prospects.

items.title: Tata Motors PV shares plunge 10% after weaker than expected JLR FY27 guidance
items.summary: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles shares dropped significantly. This followed Jaguar Land Rover's investor day where its financial outlook for fiscal year 2027 was revealed. Profitability guidance fell short of analyst expectations. JLR projects revenue growth and improved EBIT margins. North America remains a key focus for future growth. The company plans to invest in new segments and high-potential markets.

items.title: India's strategic oil reserves cover only 9-10 days of crude imports: Report
items.summary: The report released by the CEEW said other countries that rely heavily on crude imports – such as Japan and South Korea – maintain reserves sufficient for over 200 days

items.title: Sensex jumps 347 points as bull run extends to 4th day; Nifty above 24,000: 10 key highlights from stock market today
items.summary: The 30-pack Sensex closed the day at 77,156, up 347 points or 0.45% and Nifty 50 closed 97 points or 0.40% higher at 24,086.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei closes at record high near 70,000 on AI boost
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei share index hit a new record high for the third day running on Wednesday. The index closed just below the 70,000 mark. Easing Middle East conflict worries and strong buying in AI stocks boosted the market. Investors are also awaiting the US Federal Reserve's policy decision. Oil prices saw a decline as a US-Iran deal emerged.

items.title: Why a strong El Niño leaves tropical commodities acutely exposed
items.summary: Forecasters anticipate a strong El Nino developing later this year, likely boosting global temperatures and disrupting rainfall patterns. This poses significant risks to soft commodities like cocoa, coffee, and sugar, particularly those grown in tropical regions. Past El Nino events have historically led to reduced yields and price surges for these crops.

items.title: Australia shares close at two‑month high on Middle East optimism
items.summary: The Australian stock market experienced a remarkable uplift on Wednesday, reaching a two-month peak, thanks to the impressive performances of mining and banking stocks. Investors breathed a sigh of relief as news of a tentative agreement aimed at resolving the conflict in the Middle East emerged.

items.title: Global Markets| European shares flat as investors await Iran deal details, Fed policy; BMW slides
items.summary: European markets held firm today, with investors keenly awaiting developments regarding potential peace talks between the U.S. and Iran, alongside insights into the Federal Reserve's monetary policy. items.title: Lutnick’s Letter to Anthropic Warned of Curbs on Top AI Models
items.summary: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick warned Anthropic PBC in a letter last week that it would need government permission to grant foreign nationals access to its most advanced AI models and threatened criminal and civil penalties if the firm failed to comply, according to a copy obtained by Bloomberg News.

items.title: States told to prepare contingency plans for districts likely to face low rainfall during kharif season
items.summary: India faces its weakest monsoon in 11 years. Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has instructed states to prepare contingency plans for districts likely to experience low or uneven rainfall this kharif season. The government is focusing on water conservation and alternative cropping patterns. Efforts are also underway to boost cotton production through scientific farming.

items.title: The new oil? Inside the effort to turn AI computing power into a tradeable commodity
items.summary: AI compute futures could eventually rival some of the world's largest commodity markets, Silicon Data's Carmen Li believes.

items.title: CAG asks states to disclose off-budget borrowings in A/Cs
items.summary: India's Comptroller and Auditor General, K Sanjay Murthy, urged state governments to fully disclose their off-budget borrowings. He highlighted that these liabilities have reached ₹90.51 lakh crore. States are advised to reduce these debts over time. Accurate reporting is crucial for understanding the true scale of state finances and ensuring fiscal transparency. This will aid informed decision-making by policymakers.

items.title: Finmin to engage with states over looming El Nino fallout
items.summary: The Finance Ministry will soon meet with states and central ministries to assess the economic and fiscal impact of El Nino. This review will focus on potential stress on agriculture, food inflation, and increased subsidy outgo. The government is preparing relief packages for vulnerable districts. This proactive approach aims to ensure adequate supplies and mitigate inflation spikes.

items.title: KIMS offers 20 mn fresh shares to institutions in a bid to raise ₹1,500 crore
items.summary: KIMS launched a ₹1,500 crore QIP on Tuesday, offering 19.86 million fresh shares at ₹755 apiece—a 3% discount to market price. Most of the proceeds will retire debt at the listed entity and three subsidiaries, with listing of new shares expected by 25 June.

items.title: India Inc's sales growth gathers pace in March quarter, led by autos and metals: RBI data
items.summary: Manufacturing companies saw sales jump 14.5 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025-26. Automobiles, electrical machinery, and non-ferrous metals led this growth. Overall, listed private non-financial companies achieved double-digit sales growth. Raw material expenses increased, indicating input cost pressure. Staff costs for manufacturing companies moderated. Services sector companies experienced a moderation in operating profit margins.

items.title: States' liabilities rise to ₹90.5 tn in FY25, many breach fiscal gap limit: CAG
items.summary: As of 31 March 2025, total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. Public debt has more than tripled over the past decade, rising from ₹23.92 trillion in 2015-16, reflecting growing borrowing requirements across states.

items.title: SpaceX tops Amazon and Microsoft in market cap, becomes fourth-largest US company
items.summary: SpaceX surpassed both Amazon and Microsoft in market cap on Tuesday, becoming the fourth-largest US company as shares surged 13% on its third day of public trading.

items.title: GIC Re offer for sale subscribed 3.72 times on day 1; govt to offer additional 3% stake
items.summary: The expanded 5% divestment could raise up to ₹3,075 crore for the Centre as the bidding shifts to retail investors and employees on Wednesday.

items.title: Swiggy cofounders pick up stake in India’s first space unicorn Skyroot
items.summary: New Delhi: Three weeks after Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace became India’s first space unicorn, the startup’s cap-table found two new stakeholders that, for the past 12 years, have been among the most familiar names of India’s startups ecosystem.

items.title: Amid tough times, Jaguar Land Rover pays record ₹4,660-crore dividend to Tata Motors PV
items.summary: The ₹4,660-crore payout was funded by the British carmaker's strong FY25 earnings, helping cushion its Indian parent company even as production hits led to an operational loss in FY26.

items.summary: Yum Brands will sell Pizza Hut for $2.7 billion as heightened competition and cautious consumer spending is affecting the fast-food sector. Details here.

items.title: India, Sri Lanka collaborate to strengthen trade interactions through local currency settlement initiatives
items.summary: India and Sri Lanka are exploring ways to settle trade using their own currencies. This move aims to cut transaction costs and shield businesses from fluctuating US dollar values. The initiative will help Sri Lanka conserve its dollar reserves. Indian banks can now offer loans in rupees to Sri Lankan importers.

items.title: States' expenditure rises 131% in 10 yrs on welfare, development: CAG Report
items.summary: States' spending surged 131 percent from 2015-16 to 2024-25. Welfare and development drove this growth. Revenue expenditure remained the largest budget component. Committed expenses and subsidies consumed over half of revenue spending. Total state expenditure reached Rs 51.20 lakh crore in 2024-25. Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.

items.title: India's rice stocks climb to record high; wheat inventories at five-year peak
items.summary: India's government warehouses hold record rice stocks, up 15% from last year. Wheat inventories are at a five-year peak. These abundant reserves will support increased rice exports. Higher wheat stocks will help manage prices by allowing open market sales. Record production in the last crop year fueled these impressive inventories. India is well-positioned for both exports and domestic price stability.

items.summary: Protein as a category has been gaining momentum in India, with 2,672 nutrition-focused food startups currently operating in the country.

items.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to delay sowing as monsoon rains fall short
items.summary: Maharashtra is experiencing a significant rainfall deficit in early June. The state has received only 26 percent of its normal rainfall. This has led the government to advise farmers against rushing with sowing operations. Preparatory agricultural activities are ongoing. Improved rainfall is anticipated from June 18 onwards in some regions. A heatwave is also expected in Vidarbha.

items.title: AI app market shift? Anthropic's Claude earns $2.76 per user against ChatGPT's $1.74, finds report
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude is outperforming ChatGPT on revenue per user and subscriber conversion even as OpenAI's app reaches 1 billion monthly active users.

items.title: India’s gold hunger stays unshaken as higher duties fail to close the import floodgates
items.summary: India's insatiable thirst for gold remains resilient even in the face of increased import duties. Prior to the tax increase, there was a notable spike in demand, which has since sustained momentum. This pattern implies that heightened taxation is failing to temper the nation’s gold obsession.

items.title: PM Narendra Modi lines up middle-power talks ahead of Trump meeting at G7
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will attend the G7 summit in France. He will highlight India's expanding relationships with countries like Canada, the UK, and the UAE. These meetings precede his first in-person discussion with US President Donald Trump in over a year. The talks aim to strengthen India's global partnerships and economic standing.

items.title: UP, Gujarat, Jharkhand, 10 other states record revenue surplus in FY25: CAG report
items.summary: Fifteen Indian states faced revenue deficits in the fiscal year 2024-25. Thirteen states, however, reported revenue surpluses. The Comptroller and Auditor General's report highlights these financial outcomes. States' own tax revenues are growing, forming a significant part of total receipts. Committed expenditures like salaries and pensions remain a major cost for states.

items.title: India's structural growth story remains strong despite global uncertainties: Ventura
items.summary: India's long-term growth outlook remains robust despite global geopolitical uncertainties and intermittent market volatility, supported by resilient domestic demand, infrastructure investments and improving corporate fundamentals, according to financial services firm Ventura.

items.title: How Elon Musk's second-in-command Gwynne Shotwell helped turn SpaceX into an IPO giant
items.summary: CNBC spoke to people who've worked with SpaceX's president and chief operating officer.

items.title: El Nino to hit 9-10 states severely; Chouhan calls for district-level contingency plans for Kharif
items.summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has directed coordinated meetings with district officials and agricultural agencies in 9-10 states likely to face severe El Nino impacts during the Kharif season. The focus is on advance contingency planning for rain-deficient districts, increasing cotton and pulse acreage, and ensuring farmers receive scientific, solution-oriented advice.

items.title: Global commodity prices likely to remain elevated amid easing West Asia tensions; India's WPI seen at 7-8% in FY27: Report
items.summary: Global commodity prices will stay high in coming months. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia and bad weather affecting crops are the main reasons. Supply chains will take time to recover even after conflicts end. This will impact energy, metals, fertilizers, and food prices. India's inflation is expected to average 7-8 percent in FY27.

items.title: Cooling crude may ease inflation heat, give RBI room to stay on pause despite WPI surge: YES Bank
items.summary: India's wholesale inflation is high, driven by fuel and manufacturing costs. However, falling global crude oil prices and a stable rupee offer potential relief. This could allow the Reserve Bank of India to postpone interest rate increases. The central bank is monitoring weather-related food inflation risks. A new Producer Price Index framework is also being introduced.

items.title: Indian soybean acreage expected to rise on higher prices, weak monsoon outlook
items.summary: Indian farmers are planting more soybeans this year. Higher prices for soybeans are making them more profitable than corn. Forecasts of less rain also favor soybeans, which need less water than sugarcane and corn. This shift could boost India's soybean production and reduce the need for imported edible oils. Farmers are anticipating a good season for the oilseed crop.

items.title: Fiscal stress deepens across states as all 28 post deficits in FY25: CAG report
items.summary: India's states continued to face mounting fiscal pressures in 2024-25, with all 28 states reporting fiscal deficits and their combined liabilities rising to Rs 90.51 lakh crore as of March 31, 2025, according to the Comptroller and Auditor General's (CAG) report State Finances 2024-25 released on Monday.

items.title: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
items.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

items.summary: Biocon, currently the third-largest insulin supplier by volume, has invested “big time” in ramping up production capacity, Chairperson Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw says.

items.summary: India’s export growth made a dramatic turnaround in April and May. This may be more of a price-led illusion and less of a sustainable trend.

items.summary: The US-Iran peace agreement raises hopes for smoother shipping in the Gulf, yet major lines Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. remain hesitant. The industry awaits essential security assurances before resuming operations as costs surge due to the ongoing maritime conflict.

items.title: How quick commerce is helping consumer startups grow faster than ever
items.summary: Rise of quick-commerce platforms such as Blinkit, Zepto and Swiggy Instamart, alongside creator-led marketing, and a more mature startup ecosystem have helped brands achieve the revenue milestones of ₹100 crore quickly.

items.summary: Helium-3 is expensive and demand is forecast to soar, so some are planning to mine it on the moon.
